answer: The official capital city of Australia is Canberra, located in the Australian Capital Territory. It was chosen in 1908 to balance regional interests between Sydney and Melbourne. Canberra is about 150 miles southwest of Sydney.

results.snippet: # What is the capital of Australia? **The capital of Australia is Canberra**. Even though the grand opening ceremony was to be held in Melbourne, **no city had yet been designated as the capital of Australia**. Faced with widespread chaos, a radical decision was made to cut to the chase: they **would build a new city from the ground up!** Australia’s first capital city built from the ground up. ## Canberra, Australia’s small capital city. But entertaining highlights were not limited to those years in Australia’s new capital city. results.title: Where is Canberra
results.snippet: # Where is Canberra. # Where is Canberra. Canberra is Australia’s capital city. You’ll find us in south east Australia, in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Australia’s two most populated cities are nearby – Sydney to the north and Melbourne to the south. In 1901, this region was chosen as the setting for Australia’s new capital city. Australia was – and is – a young nation, and wanted a new, modern city as its capital. Canberra and it’s surrounding regions combined make a population of nearer to 950,000. As connected with the world as we are detached from the bustle and rushing you’ll find in Australia’s bigger cities. But over time, and as Canberra has emerged, the work here has changed. ## Life in Canberra. No matter where you live in Canberra, it’s easy to find your way to nature. You can also fly in from Australia’s other capital cities and regional centres or book a seat on a coach or rail service from Sydney or Melbourne.

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts | Britannica
results.snippet: Canberra is the federal capital of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory in southeastern Australia and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney. The name Canberra is a derivation of an Aboriginal term meaning “meeting place.” The city evolved from a small squatters’ settlement of stockmen which was there as early as 1824, called Canberry or Canbury. American architect Walter Burley Griffin won a worldwide competition launched in 1911 to find a design for Canberra, Australia’s new federal capital. **Canberra**, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory (ACT), in southeastern Australia, and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney. Canberra lies astride the Molonglo River, which is a tributary of the Murrumbidgee River. A worldwide competition was launched in 1911 to find a design for a new federal capital, and the winning plan was submitted by the American architect Walter Burley Griffin.
